title: Comply to Kubernetes specifications for annotation size.
body:
An annotation is a pair of key-value. The key has two parts, viz. a name and an optional prefix in DNS format.

The limitations on name is 63, prefix 253 chars. The limitation on total size of all key+value pairs combined is 256KB.

https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/annotations/#syntax-and-character-set

Fixes: https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/21663

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]". That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change? Yes

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
BREAKING: podman kube commands now comply to Kubernetes specifications for annotations' syntax, character set, and size limitations. The --no-trunc option is deprecated from the generate and play commands.
```

comments.body:
Have created this initial PR based on what Kubernetes does.
Not sure what --no-trunc (aka long options) would mean now after this change.
Looking forward for feedback from the maintainers about this PR and the need of truncate option.
Ephemeral COPR build failed. @containers/packit-build please check.
Are we still testing annotations > MAX SIze?

> Are we still testing annotations > MAX SIze?

I will remove the test cases. Do we need to remove the ‘—no-trunc’ CLI option too or is it okay to leave it there?
do not remove e2e and system tests.  enhance them.

> do not remove e2e and system tests. enhance them.

Could you elaborate on “enhance”?

> > do not remove e2e and system tests. enhance them.
> 
> Could you elaborate on “enhance”?

expand them based on what you change or add

Cockpit tests failed for commit b7a0e4ea5343cc2b189a70cae36f36afcd0bd415. @martinpitt, @jelly, @mvollmer please check.
The release note has to note that this is a breaking change.

In general I'm OK with adding this, given we haven't branched 5.0 or declared feature complete, but it will have to be merged soon, ideally this week, as I want to call the parts of Podman that are not machine stable after RC3 on Thursday.
Code LGTM
All tests passed except one sys test that failed in teardown for unable to stop containers timely. Seems an unrelated issue.

/lgtm
/hold

> > Flag --no-trunc has been deprecated, podman kube complies to Kubernetes annotation specifications
> 
> Am I the only one that finds this message confusing? As a user I have no idea what this means and what I am supposed to do without consulting further docs. Any reason to not just turn the flag into a NOP and ignore it silently?

If the intention is to remove the flag in future, then printing the "deprecated" message will help users to remove it from their usage. Anyways, the unintended behavior of annotation limitations in podman is getting updated to a standard specification. The "kube" users using the podman specific flag should know the Kubernetes specification and what they should expect hereon.

I will leave it to you and other maintainers to decide whether any further code change is needed. I will not have access to dev env from tomorrow until March 7. If you can let me know _today_ the exact change you expect, I will try to incorporate. Otherwise, feel free to edit this PR as appropriate.
Removed the Deprecate() function call to make the `--no-trunc` flag a silent no-op.
/lgtm
Yes, the flag is already made hidden.
Flake machine-hyper?
/hold cancel
